zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Hammer是Sir Walter Bredwardine的女兒，於大約1357年出生在威爾士福林爾。她和Owain Glyndwr ap Gruffud Prince of Wales至少有1個女兒。她去世於1420年在英國賀福爾享年64歲，埋葬在英國賀福爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Margaret Hammer是Sir Walter Bredwardine的女儿，于大约1357年出生在威尔士福林爾。她和Owain Glyndwr ap Gruffud Prince of Wales至少有1个女儿。她去世于1420年在英国賀福爾享年64岁，埋葬在英国賀福爾。

nameMeanings.name: Hammer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, English, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from Middle High German hamer, Yiddish hamer, a metonymic occupational name for a maker or user of hammers, for example in a forge, or a nickname for a forceful person. As an English surname, the derivation from Middle English ham(m)er, hamor ‘hammer’ (Old English hamor) is formally possible, either as a metonymic occupational name or as a locative or occupational name taken from a shop sign or inn sign. However, there is no evidence that such appellations became hereditary surnames. The surname of German origin (possibly also in the sense 2 below) is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).

English and German: topographic name for someone who lived in an area of water meadow, or flat, low-lying alluvial land beside a stream, Middle English ham(me), Old English hamm, Old High German ham (see Hamm ) + the English and German agent suffix -er. In England, names composed of a topographic term + -er are characteristic of southern England, especially Kent, Sussex, Surrey, and Hampshire.
English: possibly a variant of   Hanmer  , and in northern England a variant of   Hamer  .

variants.name: Hemmerling
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hartmut, Klaus.
German: diminutive of   Hammer  .
Dutch: derivative of   Hemmerich   2.

variants.name: Haemmerle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Florian, Fritz, Manfred.
South German (Hämmerle): diminutive of Hammer 1. Compare Hammerle .

variants.name: Hammers
meanings: German and Dutch: genitivized form of   Hammer   2.

variants.name: Hommer
meanings: German: altered form of   Hammer  .

variants.name: Hamernik
meanings: Czech (Hamerník): occupational name for a smith, an agent derivative of hamr ‘forge’, which is from German Hammer ‘hammer’ (see Hammer ).

variants.name: Hammerman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Chaim, Hillel, Sheina.
Americanized form of German Hammermann, and a variant of the same Jewish (Ashkenazic) surname: variant of Hammer 1, with the addition of Middle High German man, German Mann ‘man’.

variants.name: Hemmerle
meanings: German: diminutive of Hammer . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), in the form Hemmerlé.

variants.name: Hamm
meanings: English (London): topographic name for someone who lived at a place called from Middle English ham(me), hom(me) (Old English hamm), which meant ‘land in a river bend’, ‘land hemmed in by marshland’, ‘wet land hemmed in by higher ground’, ‘river meadow’, or ‘cultivated plot on the edge of woodland or moor’. The topographic term is found mainly in the South Midlands and southern England. There are many farmsteads with this name in Devon and Sussex, five more substantial settlements called Ham or Hamp in Somerset, as well as East and West Ham in Essex, and places called Ham in Gloucestershire, Hampshire, Kent, Surrey, and Wiltshire. This form of the surname is also comparatively frequent in Ireland.
German: topographic name for someone who lived on land in a river bend, Old High German ham (see 1 above).
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from any of numerous places called Hamm, mainly the city in Westphalia.

variants.name: Hammerle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Ewald, Fritz, Konrad, Waltraud.
South German (also Hämmerle): diminutive of Hammer 1. Compare Haemmerle .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Hammer was born about 1357, in Flintshire, Wales as the daughter of Sir Walter Bredwardine. She had at least 1 daughter with Owain Glyndwr ap Gruffud Prince of Wales. She died in 1420, in Herefordshire, England, at the age of 64, and was buried in Herefordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Hammer est née environ 1357, à Flintshire, Galles, fille de Sir Walter Bredwardine. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Owain Glyndwr ap Gruffud Prince of Wales. Elle est décédée en 1420, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterrée à Herefordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Hammer wurde um 1357 in Flintshire, Wales, als Tochter von Sir Walter Bredwardine geboren. Sie hatte mit Owain Glyndwr ap Gruffud Prince of Wales mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1420 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Herefordshire, England und wurde in Herefordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret Hammer è nata il circa 1357 nel Flintshire, Galles come figlia di Sir Walter Bredwardine. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Owain Glyndwr ap Gruffud Prince of Wales. Morì nel 1420, nel Herefordshire, England, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolta a Herefordshire, England.

ja.Pembrokeshire, Wales: ウェールズペンブロケシュアイア
ja.Herefordshire, England: イギリスハーエフォードシュアイア
ja.Flintshire, Wales: ウェールズフリントシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Hammerは約1357年にウェールズフリントシュアイアでSir Walter Bredwardineの娘として生まれた。彼女はOwain Glyndwr ap Gruffud Prince of Walesとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1420年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアで64歳で死去し，イギリスハーエフォードシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Hammer(은)는 대략 1357년 웨일스 플린츠히르에서 Sir Walter Bredwardine의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Owain Glyndwr ap Gruffud Prince of Wales(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1420년 영국 허퍼드실르에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 허퍼드실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret Hammer nasceu aproximadamente 1357, em Flintshire, Gales filha de Sir Walter Bredwardine. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com Owain Glyndwr ap Gruffud Prince of Wales. Ela faleceu em 1420, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra, com 64 anos, e foi sepultada em Herefordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Hammer родилась около 1357 г. в местности флинцхир, Уэльс. Её отца звали Sir Walter Bredwardine. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Owain Glyndwr ap Gruffud Prince of Wales. Она умерла в 1420 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия в возрасте 64 лет и была похоронена в местности хирефордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret Hammer nació aproximadamente 1357, en Flintshire, Gales como hija de Sir Walter Bredwardine. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Owain Glyndwr ap Gruffud Prince of Wales. Murió en 1420, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultada en Herefordshire, Inglaterra.
